Ankita Prashant Naik
Data Scientist · ML Engineer
Email LinkedIn GitHub

Hi, I'm Ankita, a recent MS in Data Science graduate from Indiana University Bloomington with a Bachelor's in Information Science & Engineering. My experience spans AI, machine learning, NLP, data engineering, and analytics across research and industry projects. At Project 990, I built Python and SQL pipelines processing 1.5M+ IRS records and a BART-based NLP workflow classifying 180K nonprofit mission statements. At Global Health Impact, I automated healthcare models across 217 countries, replacing manual Excel workflows with reproducible pipelines. I have also built AI-powered wellness apps, multi-agent LLM research workflows, and interactive dashboards on 20M+ record datasets. What excites me most is solving problems through a mix of analytical thinking, creativity, and technology.

Selected Work

Projects

MAR 2026
Online Retail Customer Analytics

Identified Champions as the highest-value customer segment (69% of net revenue) through RFM segmentation and cohort retention analysis on UK online retail data. Built an interpretable re-purchase risk model (ROC-AUC 0.69) to surface 13 high-value, high-risk customers for targeted retention.

OCT 2025
DailyBloom AI: Wellness Agent Chatbot

Full-stack AI wellness companion with personalized lifestyle suggestions and interactive progress tracking — habit streaks, wellness scoring, and visualizations based on daily inputs across sleep, hydration, meals, and activity.

PythonStreamlitSQLiteAnthropic APIClaude Sonnet 4.5
View on GitHub
NOV 2025
AI Agent for Deep Research Workflow

Architected a 3-agent LLM workflow (research → writer → evaluator) using LlamaIndex, GPT-4o-mini, and Tavily web search for structured research report generation — with custom event-driven orchestration and self-reflection loops for iterative refinement.

LlamaIndexGPT-4o-miniTavilyMulti-agentRAG
2024
Spatio-Temporal Analysis of Air Quality and Health Outcomes in California

Engineered a robust data pipeline in R aggregating ten years of daily fine particulate matter and ground-level ozone measurements into comprehensive county-level datasets. Conducted spatio-temporal EDA to isolate regional pollution trends and seasonal concentration spikes driven by wildfire events. Developed multiple linear regression models evaluating the association between pollution exposure and asthma-related ED visit rates — improving R² from 0.007 to 0.537 by integrating socio-economic features including poverty rates and median household income.

Rdplyrtidyrggplot2RegressionBICSpatio-Temporal
View on GitHub
FEB – MAY 2025
IndyGo On-Time Performance Dashboard

Interactive Power BI dashboard analyzing 20.6M+ IndyGo transit records (2022–2024). Surfaced insights on ridership growth, peak periods, busiest/least-busy stops and high-usage routes to support service planning and resource allocation.

Power BISQLTransit AnalyticsDAX
JAN – MAY 2025
MindStat: Mental Health Analytics Platform

Engineered dynamic data dashboards and an interactive analytics application in a team environment using Python to visualize and extract insights from mental health datasets. Designed the underlying data infrastructure utilizing Flask and MongoDB, optimizing NoSQL storage to ensure rapid, scalable data retrieval for real-time analysis.

PythonFlaskMongoDBData VisualizationNoSQL

Work History

Experience

AUG 2025 – PRESENT
Data Scientist
Project 990 · Bloomington, Indiana
  • Built a BART-based zero-shot NLP pipeline in Python to classify ~180K IRS 990-EZ nonprofit mission statements and generate faith-specific features for foundation network mapping across the United States.
  • Built an end-to-end data pipeline using Python and SQL to process 1.5M+ IRS Form 990 financial records, transforming raw filings into a standardized schema loaded into a MySQL database for downstream analytics.
  • Conducted causal analysis using stepwise regression to evaluate the impact of philanthropic grants on food insecurity; adjusted for confounding variables within community-level datasets to isolate the true treatment effect of the funding.
  • Optimized the analytical pipeline's visual outputs by redesigning bar charts, box plots, and scatter views; implemented normalized comparisons and strategic outlier separation to improve the interpretability of complex, skewed data distributions.
JAN 2026 – MAY 2026
Data Science Research Assistant
Global Health Impact · Bloomington, Indiana
  • Designed and executed an ETL pipeline in Python (pandas, NumPy) to extract, clean, and transform drug-disease efficacy data across 217 countries, replacing manual Excel-based workflows with reproducible, scalable data models.
  • Engineered Global Health Impact Index computations across multiple disease and drug intervention datasets, applying validation and transformation logic to ensure consistent, auditable results.
  • Automated drug efficacy calculations with structured normalization steps, feeding directly into the Global Health Impact forecasting tool used for cross-country health analysis.
